semantic.role: Pipeline task stage responsible for computing reverse dependency edges by scanning tracked source files for index documents and caching the resulting dependency graph.
responsibilities: Retrieve all tracked files from the scanner context.
For each tracked file, read its index document if present and compatible.
Extract source path and target path from each index document to build reverse dependency edges.
Cache reverse edges in an in-memory map indexed by target path.
Report warnings for files without a compatible index document via `reportIndexReadIssueOnce`.
Override `exec` method to orchestrate the iterative scanning and edge-building.
Provide checkpoint ID 'reverse-index' for pipeline resumption.
outOfScope: File content analysis or AST extraction beyond index document reading.
CLI command parsing or any user-facing I/O.
Orchestration of services outside the core task contract (e.g., database writes, MCP tools).
Writing or updating index documents; it is a read-only consumer of the index cache.
invariants: The task must only depend on core contracts (`SpineTask`, `TaskContext`) and index-reader utilities.
Reverse edges are built purely from the index documents of tracked files, not from dynamic analysis.
The task must not modify the scanner state or manifest; it only reads from them.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: To compute a reverse dependency graph from existing index documents, enabling downstream views (e.g., change-impact, risk-hotspots) to assess which files depend on a given module.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: The recent commit 'refactor: remove legacy CLI aliases, rebuild docs, fix schema and tests' does not modify this file's public contract or implementation logic; minimal impact.

publicSurface.description: Class that extends SpineTask to scan tracked files and build a reverse dependency index. Executed as a pipeline stage; exports its results via the checkpointed state for downstream views.
